t_ch: 咳咳，马老师的小课堂开课了，有问题的同学记得随时提问。
t_en: Ahem—ahem! Miss Nihilux's mini-class is officially in session. If you have any questions, feel free to ask at any time.

t_ch: 马老师，为什么选星穹列车做漫画主题？
t_en: Miss Colt, why did you pick the Astral Express as the theme for your comic?

t_ch: 因为我是现实取材派，但遗憾的是，我之前画的《黄泉弑神斩》、《巡海游侠战记》还有其余几十本漫画都腰斩了。
t_en: Well, I'm all about realism, but sadly, my previous works like Acheron's Godslay, Galaxy Rangers' Saga, and dozens of others all got canceled.

t_ch: 如果不是你们打败了铁墓，热度飙升，这本也要腰斩。下次不要问这么悲伤的问题。
t_en: If you hadn't beaten Irontomb and boosted its popularity, this one would've been canceled too. Let's not ask sad questions, alright?

t_ch: 对、对不起！
t_en: S—Sorry!

t_ch: 注意看，画面中间的小动物叫球棒浣熊，是《苍天航路绒绒号》的主角。不久前，三月兔和汪汪丹在一个垃圾桶里找到了{F#她}{M#他}。
t_en: Alright, pay attention. The cute little critter in the center of the image is Baseball Raccoon, the main character of Fluffy Across the Blue. Just recently, March Bunny and Woof Woof Dan found {M#him}{F#her} in a trashcan.

t_ch: 此时，一只恐怖的怪兽正在空间站肆虐，它向球棒浣熊喷出火焰，但火焰没能穿透垃圾桶，反而点燃了垃圾，弥漫的臭味熏倒了怪兽。
t_en: At that moment, a terrifying monster is causing havoc on the space station. It tries to spit fire at Baseball Raccoon, but the flames don't burn through the trashcan. Instead, it sets the trash on fire, and the stench knocks the monster out.

t_ch: 马老师！为什么{F#她}{M#他}是主角？
t_en: Miss Colt, why is {M#he}{F#she} the protagonist?

t_ch: 现在的读者都讲究一个代入感，这种对过去一无所知的角色，当然是最好的人选。
t_en: Readers today value immersion, and a character who has no memory of their past is, of course, the best choice.

t_ch: 我、我也对过去一无所知，怎么不选我？
t_en: I don't know anything about my past either, so why didn't you pick me?

t_ch: 虽然{NICKNAME}上车最晚，但{F#她}{M#他}在各个世界留下的段子太多了，就连罗浮的监狱里都有{F#她}{M#他}殴打外星生物的传言。
t_en: Even though {NICKNAME} joined the Express the latest, {M#he's}{F#she's} left behind so many stories across different worlds that there are even rumors in the Luofu's prison about {M#him}{F#her} beating up alien lifeforms.

t_ch: 这么神经的素材不拿来做主角，我同意，阿哈都不同意。
t_en: With material this crazy, not making it the main character? I might disagree, but Aha absolutely wouldn't

t_ch: 三月，我把主角让给你一天。
t_en: March, I'll give you the protagonist role for a day.

t_ch: 当好你的球棒浣熊吧，本姑娘用不着谁来让。
t_en: Stick to being the Baseball Raccoon okay? I don't need special treatment.

t_ch: 马老师，你做得好啊！
t_en: Miss Colt, well said!

t_ch: 哇，这幅得意忘形的嘴脸。
t_en: Wow, that's a smug face!

t_ch: 好现实的选角标准。
t_en: Well, that's the reality of casting for you.

t_ch: 原来你每次一落地就到处乱跑，是为了提高自己的出镜率！
t_en: So every time you land somewhere, you just run around to get more screen time, huh!?

t_ch: 三月小姐，绒绒号只是本漫画。而在此之外，每个角色、作者和读者，我们不都是自己人生的主角吗？
t_en: March, The Fluffy is just a comic. Outside of this, the author and the readers, every character in this world, aren't we all the protagonists of our own lives?

t_ch: 太热血了，马老师，我完全明白了。
t_en: That sounds so passionate, Miss Colt! I totally get it now.

t_ch: 于是，脱线的球棒浣熊，寡言的汪汪丹，以及活泼可爱的三月兔，组成了大家喜闻乐见的三人小队。
t_en: And so, the unruly Baseball Raccoon, the quiet Woof Woof Dan, and the lively March Bunny formed a trio everyone loves.

t_ch: 其实我也有个问题。
t_en: Actually, I have a question, too.

t_ch: 为什么大家都是小动物？
t_en: Why are they all small animals?

t_ch: 自从吃了销量的铁拳，我就参透了一个宇宙至理——在现代社会，还得是毛茸茸的萌物最吃香。
t_en: Ever since I got punched in the face by sales numbers, I've grasped a universal truth: In today's world, fluffy cute animals are always a crowd pleaser.

t_ch: 的确，帕姆的可爱程度，连我都自愧不如……
t_en: Seriously, even I feel like I can't compete with Pom-Pom's cuteness...

t_ch: 为了设计成年组的形象，我花了不少功夫。在打败怪兽后，球棒浣熊接受了其他小动物的邀请，登上了全是毛茸茸的绒绒号。
t_en: Designing the adult versions took some work. After defeating the monster, Baseball Raccoon accepted an invitation from the other fluffy animals and boarded The Fluffy.

t_ch: 在这里，主角见到了三只性格迥异的大动物。首先，是读者们敬爱的列车长，宇宙无敌可爱的帕姆。
t_en: Here, the protagonist meets three big animals with distinct personalities. First, the adorable and universally loved conductor, Pom-Pom.

t_ch: 在它的身旁，是优雅的领航员，姬奇猫小姐。
t_en: Beside them is the elegant Navigator, Miss Himekat.

t_ch: 我知道，这是「姬奇猫」，原型是姬子姐——噗，{NICKNAME}，你可别告诉姬子姐啊。
t_en: I know, that's Himekat. Her original inspiration is Himeko. Phew, {NICKNAME}, please don't tell her!

t_ch: 噗。
t_en: Pfft.

t_ch: 太酷了。
t_en: Awesome!

t_ch: 你的起名品味…
t_en: Your taste in naming is...

t_ch: 至于另一边这只棕色的大狗，说出他的名字，怕吓你们一跳！没错，他就是——
t_en: And the big brown dog on the other side, see? His name will shock you! That's right, he's—

t_ch: 杨叔！
t_en: Mr. Yang!

t_ch: 杨叔？
t_en: Mr. Yang?

t_ch: 没其他名字？
t_en: No fictionalized name for him?

t_ch: 就是杨叔。
t_en: Just Mr. Yang.

t_ch: 老师的起名品味确实…非同一般。
t_en: Your taste in naming is indeed... extraordinary.

t_ch: 总之，六只毛茸茸的小萌物就这样搭乘列车绒绒号，踏上了旅途。
t_en: Anyway, the six fluffy little creatures boarded The Fluffy Express and set off on their journey.

t_ch: 剧情方面应该用不着我介绍，和你们经历的大差不差，无非是针对二相乐园的受众，进行了一点点艺术加工。
t_en: As for the plot, it's pretty much the same as what you've all experienced, just with a bit of artistic tweaking to suit Planarcadia's audience.

t_ch: 一点点？
t_en: A bit?

t_ch: 亿点点。
t_en: A tiny bit.

t_ch: 艺术加工？
t_en: Artistic tweaking?

t_ch: 缝合点本地元素，大家才乐意看嘛。
t_en: If you don't stitch in a few local elements, no one's going to be interested.

t_ch: 在比幻月游戏还要残酷的漫画界，不投读者所好是很难活下来的。
t_en: In the comic world, which is even more cutthroat than the Phantasmoon Games, if you don't cater to the readers' tastes, it's nearly impossible to survive.

t_ch: 很早以前，漫画大师们可以用一百章的内容做铺垫。但放到现在，要不了一百页，作品就会被腰斩。
t_en: Once upon a time, great comic artists had the luxury of a hundred chapters to build things up. Now, if you don't hook readers within a hundred pages, you're done.

t_ch: 读者愈发难满足，作者不得不创作出更刺激、更有噱头的内容。你们不觉得，这和幻月游戏有异曲同工之妙？
t_en: Readers today are hard to please, and authors have to come up with ever more shocking and sensational material. Doesn't this remind you of the Phantasmoon Games?

t_ch: 看来你们已经完全了解绒绒号的角色了。那么接下来和你们谈谈我目前面临的创作问题吧。
t_en: Looks like you're all familiar with the characters from The Fluffy. Now, let me talk about some of the creative challenges I'm facing.

t_ch: 在这批绒绒号的立牌上，你们看到了什么？
t_en: So, what do you see on these The Fluffy stands?

t_ch: 变成可爱小动物的{NICKNAME}。
t_en: It's {NICKNAME} turned into a cute little animal.

t_ch: 一只小浣熊。
t_en: A little raccoon.

t_ch: 盗版的我。
t_en: A pirated version of me.

t_ch: 毛茸茸的我。
t_en: A furry version of me.

t_ch: 没错，这是角色。而你是{F#她}{M#他}的原型。
t_en: Yes, this is the character, and you're the inspiration behind {M#him.}{F#her.}

t_ch: 但即便是你这么有趣的原型，也一定会有许许多多读者们不关心、不想也没有义务去了解的故事。
t_en: But even for someone as interesting as you, there are many backstories that readers don't care about, don't want to know, or don't feel obliged to understand.

t_ch: 漫画家的工作就是把值得看的部分抓出来，呈现给读者。
t_en: A comic artist's job is to capture the most interesting parts and present them to the readers.

t_ch: 可爱又机灵的，就是三月兔。脱线但靠谱的，是球棒浣熊。这叫做，具体的原型和抽象的角色。
t_en: The cute and clever one is March Bunny. The unruly but reliable one is Baseball Raccoon. This is what we call a concrete archetype paired with an abstract character.

t_ch: 而角色抽象的一面，又会在读者那里被进一步简化。
t_en: And the abstract side of a character will only be further simplified in the eyes of the readers.

t_ch: 你们觉得，全宇宙的读者是怎么看待你们这些无名客的？
t_en: So, how do you think the readers across the universe view you Nameless?

t_ch: 过客。
t_en: Passersby.

t_ch: 可不要小瞧了你们的影响力哦。
t_en: Don't underestimate your influence.

t_ch: 恶霸。
t_en: Bullies.

t_ch: 那是奥斯瓦尔多吧。
t_en: That must be Oswaldo, right?

t_ch: 英雄。
t_en: Heroes.

t_ch: 说得不错。
t_en: Well said.

t_ch: 因为英雄的壮举和事迹，所以关注你们；也因为你们展现出的气质，绒绒号里的角色才找到了灵感。
t_en: Your heroic deeds and accomplishments drew my attention, and your demeanor gave me inspiration for the character in The Fluffy.

t_ch: 但英雄的身份为角色带来人气的同时，也成为了束缚角色的枷锁。所以，绒绒号的小动物们必须打倒更多强大的反派，上演更精彩的故事，才能博得观众们的满堂彩。
t_en: But while the hero identity boosts the character's popularity, it also becomes a limitation. That's why the little animals in The Fluffy have to defeat even more powerful villains and keep upping the ante to get more applause from the audience.

t_ch: 这样一来，读者的认知和期待也约束了角色的发展，要是{F#她}{M#他}做出了违背形象的行为，人气很快就会一落千丈。
t_en: In turn, the readers' perceptions and expectations constrain the character's growth. If {M#he}{F#she} acts out of line, the popularity will crash.

t_ch: 要是{F#她}{M#他}不能贡献出出人意料的点子，或是不能变得更强，观众们很快就会厌倦这部漫画的！
t_en: If {M#he}{F#she} can't come up with new ideas or grow stronger, the audience will soon lose interest in the comic!

t_ch: 很遗憾，《苍天航路绒绒号》的创作，正处于这样一个尴尬的瓶颈。
t_en: Unfortunately, the creation of Fluffy Across the Blue is now stuck at this very awkward bottleneck.

t_ch: 实不相瞒，画了这么久的漫画，我肚子里的货也差不多见底了。
t_en: Honestly, after drawing comics for so long, I've pretty much run out of ideas.

t_ch: 几百个琥珀纪前，人们会因为看到一只后空翻的猫开怀大笑。
t_en: Hundreds of Amber Eras ago, people would laugh out loud just from seeing a cat do a backflip.

t_ch: 现在，想激起同样热烈的笑声，恐怕得让这只猫在珠星大厦和机铠一对一死斗。
t_en: Now, to get the same kind of enthusiastic reaction, you'd probably need to have that cat fight a mechatron in a one-on-one showdown in Pearluxe Tower.

t_ch: 虽然绒绒号的日常特刊大卖特卖，但一直画番外篇，不推进主线也不是办法。
t_en: Although The Fluffy specials are selling like crazy, doing side stories without progressing the main plot isn't a long-term solution.

t_ch: 自从雇佣的八卦忆者全部折在翁法罗斯，我的素材来源就越来越少。
t_en: Ever since all the gossip Memokeepers I hired got wiped out in Amphoreus, my source of intel's been drying up fast.

t_ch: 但是嘛，如果有星穹列车的内部人员，悄悄地为我提供一些幻月游戏内部的秘辛，让读者在看最新话的时候，忍不住惊叹——
t_en: But if someone from the Astral Express could quietly feed me a few juicy insider secrets from the Phantasmoon Games, imagine the readers' reaction when the next chapter drops—

t_ch: 「天哪！难道模糊二维马老师有时光机吗？」
t_en: "Whoa! Does Blurry_QR_Colt have a time machine or what!?"

t_ch: 这样一来，绒绒号未来的销量就有救了。
t_en: That'd totally save The Fluffy's future sales.

t_ch: 反正我手下的狸猫崽子已经是{NICKNAME}的员工，让它们把幻月游戏的新闻稿顺便给我一份，就当是买下了你们的独家改编权。
t_en: After all, my little furbo crew already works for {NICKNAME}. Just have them slip me a copy of the Phantasmoon Games press release. Let's call it buying your exclusive adaptation rights.

t_ch: 到时候，我们五五分成。
t_en: We'll do a fifty-fifty split.

t_ch: 成交。
t_en: Deal.

t_ch: 嘿嘿，我都想好版权费怎么花了。首先给帕姆买十件新衣服，给姬子买台新的咖啡机，然后……
t_en: Heh, I've already figured out how to spend the royalties. First, I'll buy Pom-Pom ten new outfits, then get Himeko a brand-new coffee machine, and after that...

t_ch: 我再考虑考虑。
t_en: I'll have to think about it.

t_ch: 三七分成！
t_en: What about a thirty-seventy split!?

t_ch: 成交。
t_en: Deal.

t_ch: 是你三我七喔！
t_en: Seventy for me, thirty for you!

t_ch: 哇，你变脸变得比花火还快。
t_en: Wow, you change your face faster than Sparkle.

t_ch: 好了，既然大家把分成都谈妥了，创作瓶颈的解决也指日可待。就尽情期待「星铁FES」虚照老师真人登场吧！
t_en: Alright, since we've got the profit split sorted, looks like my creative block won't last much longer. Just wait for my appearance at "Star Rail FEST"!

t_ch: 「星铁FES」，一场汇聚百万双眼睛，聚集百万愿力的盛会。盛邀之下，觊觎欢愉之主赐福的种种黑暗力量，也必然会浮出水面。
t_en: "Star Rail FEST," a grand event that gathers millions of eyes and an inordinate amount of Wishpower. And under all that attention, the dark forces coveting the Master of Elation's blessing are bound to surface.

t_ch: 嘿嘿，英雄和魔鬼在乐园共舞，此时此刻，世界上还有比这更好的创作素材吗？
t_en: Hehe, heroes and devils dancing together in Planarcadia. Tell me, is there a better source of inspiration than that?

t_ch: 我的英雄，可别让大家失望哦。
t_en: My hero, don't let us down.

t_ch: 此时，在绘世学院……
t_en: Meanwhile, at the Graphia Academy...

t_ch: 「谒者是愿力的主人，但愿力也会成为束缚谒者的枷锁，迫使他们满足其愿望。」
t_en: "A Supplicant is the master of Wishpower, but Wishpower can also become the shackles that bind the Supplicant, forcing them to fulfill its desires."

t_ch: 「喜爱与厌恶是相似的，依赖与恐惧是相似的，操控愿力的不仅可以是爱，也可以是恨。」
t_en: "Love and hatred are alike. Dependence resembles fear. What commands Wishpower isn't only love, but also hatred."

t_ch: 谢谢…这些笔记应该能给{NICKNAME}提供不少帮助。
t_en: Thank you... These notes will really be a big help to {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 那就好，能帮到你们就好。
t_en: Good. I'm so glad to be of help.

t_ch: 我们家族历代有不少人都尝试过加入幻月游戏，想要依仗星神的伟力解除困扰绘世血脉的诅咒。
t_en: For generations, many members of our family have attempted to enter the Phantasmoon Games, hoping to rely on the Aeon's immense power to lift the curse afflicting the Graphia bloodline.

t_ch: 他们有的与面具失之交臂，只能作为观测者见证历史。有的佩戴上面具，却最终功败垂成。
t_en: Some came close to the Mask, but could only watch as history passed them by. Others wore it, only to fail in the end.

t_ch: 直到四百年前，你的曾曾外祖父，弘赢得了幻月游戏的胜利，成功谒见了阿哈。
t_en: That was, until four hundred years ago when your great-great-grandfather, Hiro, won the Phantasmoon Games and successfully gained an audience with Aha.

t_ch: 但，家族的诅咒并未断绝。他的笔记中这样写道——
t_en: But the family's curse was not broken. In his notes, he wrote—

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>「正如毁灭之神无法手捧花束，欢愉之神亦对诅咒无计可施，此为因果循环。」</color>
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>"Just as the God of Destruction cannot cradle a bouquet, so too is the God of Elation powerless against a curse, such is the cycle of cause and effect."</color>

t_ch: 父亲，你是不是想问，是什么医好了我……
t_en: Father... are you wondering what it was that healed me?

t_ch: 我知道你有秘密瞒着我，但我不在乎十五年前是什么治好了你，只要你活着，我可以等到你愿意告诉我的那天。
t_en: I know you're keeping something from me. But I don't care what cured you fifteen years ago. As long as you're alive, I can wait until the day you're ready to tell me.

t_ch: 但有些话，即使你会厌烦，身为父亲，我也不得不说。
t_en: Still, as a father, there are things I must say, even if you don't want to hear them.

t_ch: 身为艺术家，我钟情于见证，终将消逝的生命经由历练，展露出足以铭刻在时间上的光辉瞬间。
t_en: As an artist, I'm fascinated by fleeting lives: how they struggle, suffer, and in doing so, reveal a brilliance worthy of time's memory.

t_ch: 作为不称职的父亲，我不想眼睁睁看着女儿走向悲剧。「我祝福你成为英雄，迎接和阿基维利一样的结局」，这样的话我说不出口。
t_en: But as a father, I can't stand by and watch my daughter walk into tragedy. "I bless you to be a hero, to welcome the same end as Akivili." Those are words I could never speak.

t_ch: 正因前路难测，才需要有人第一个迈出脚步，开拓出能让更多人走下去的道路。
t_en: The road ahead is shrouded by uncertainty, and that's why someone must take the first step, to trailblaze a path others can follow.

t_ch: 所以我没法阻止你，即使我无比希望你能留下来。这是我女儿的选择，我只能默默支持她，祈祷她平安归来。
t_en: I can't stop you, even though every part of me wishes you'd stay. But this is your choice, my daughter's choice. All I can do is stand by you quietly and pray you'll come back safely.

t_ch: 还记得我常讲的那个睡前故事吗？
t_en: Do you still remember the bedtime story I used to tell?

t_ch: 传说天上有一位公主，她每日每夜在花园中作画，不知人间的喜悲。
t_en: They say there was once a princess in the sky. Day and night, she painted in her celestial garden, untouched by the joys and sorrows of the mortal world.

t_ch: 一天夜里，画里传来一阵巨响，疑惑的她走入画中，穿过花海与群山，直至世界边缘。
t_en: One night, a thunderous sound echoed from her painting. Curious, she stepped inside, through seas of flowers, across mountains, until she reached the world's edge.

t_ch: 那里有一座<color=#dbc291ff>古老的高塔</color>，它贯穿天地，仿佛容纳了整个宇宙。
t_en: There, stood <color=#dbc291ff>an ancient tower</color>, so tall it pierced the heavens, so vast it seemed to hold the universe itself.

t_ch: 沿着阶梯攀援，在高塔顶端，公主看见了花园外的世界。有哭声从远方传来，那是燃烧着熊熊大火的人间。
t_en: Climbing the endless staircase, the princess reached the summit, and from there, she saw the world beyond her garden. In the distance, she heard cries, the voices of people, burning in raging fire.

t_ch: 从「无限的高塔」中降生的绘世踏入尘世，她挥动画笔创造出千百的幻造种，为对抗毁灭的战场带来了转机。
t_en: From that "Tower of Infinity" descended the {RUBY_B#Graphia}world-painter{RUBY_E#}, to walk the mortal realm. With a single stroke of her brush, she brought forth a thousand imagenae, turning the tide against Destruction.

t_ch: 去有人哭喊的地方，去有火燃烧的地方，<color=#dbc291ff>这便是我们家族的姓氏「无量塔」的由来。</color>
t_en: To go where there are cries, to go where there are flames. <color=#dbc291ff>That is the meaning of Murata.</color>

t_ch: 但除却姓氏外，对先祖名讳「绘世」的解读，也是我送给你的期许。
t_en: But beyond the family name, my interpretation of our ancestor's name, "Graphia," is also the hope I place in you.

t_ch: 姬子，无论你做出什么选择…如果这就是你想做的，那就去「绘世」吧，去用开拓的脚步描绘你眼中的世界吧。
t_en: Himeko, whatever choice you make... if this is truly what you want, then go, use those steps blazed upon trails to paint the world as you see it.

t_ch: 谢谢你，爸爸。
t_en: Thank you, Dad.

t_ch: 对了，你的身体状况…还好吗？我不在的这些年里，没有沾上烟酒之类的毛病吧？
t_en: By the way, how's your... health? You haven't picked up any bad habits like smoking or drinking while I was away, have you?

t_ch: 堂堂绘世学院的校长，还是要给学生们做表率的。就是最近腱鞘炎老是复发，腰间盘突出也越来越严重了。
t_en: Hah, as the dean of Graphia Academy, I still have to set a good example for my students. It's just... my tendinitis keeps acting up, my herniated disc is getting worse.

t_ch: 姬子，你会在这里停留多久？
t_en: Himeko, how long will you be staying?

t_ch: 至少在月相变化之前，我暂时不会离开二相乐园的。
t_en: At least until the lunar cycle shifts, I'll stay here in Planarcadia until then.

t_ch: 是吗？要是幻月永远满盈就好了……
t_en: I see. If only the Phantasmoon could stay full forever...

t_ch: 姬子，祝你旅途顺遂。
t_en: Himeko... safe travels on the trails ahead.

t_ch: 绒绒号，好绒绒，小帕姆，毛茸茸。
t_en: The Fluffy goes puff-puff-puff, Pom-Pom's fur? Extra fluffy stuff.

t_ch: 一段时间后，二维市的某处……
t_en: Some time later, somewhere in Duomension City...

t_ch: 老朋友，我不怪你，可我不想再拖着这幅残躯，像个累赘似的活下去。所以，就用这颗子弹，来弥补你欠我的那杯酒吧。
t_en: My friend, I don't blame you, but I'm done being a breathing dead weight. So do me a favor, and pay me back for that drink with this bullet.

t_ch: 动手啊！别当孬种！在我变成丰饶的怪物前，让我作为一个巡海游侠死掉！
t_en: Just do it, you coward! Let me die as a Galaxy Ranger before I turn into a monster of Abundance!

t_ch: 蕉，开枪，我，求你了。
t_en: Bana, shoot me. I, beg you.

t_ch: 天弓在上，愿你们能做个好梦。
t_en: By Skybow above, may you have a pleasant dream.

t_ch: 「不知生为何人，不知为何存在于此，这不叫生命。」
t_en: "Not knowing who you are, not knowing why you exist here... that isn't life."

t_ch: 在古老的年代里，人类每天的目标是获得两千大卡热量的食物。那时候的人类知道自己不过是动物的一员，明白自己为何迁徙、狩猎、采集……
t_en: In ancient times, humanity's daily goal was to obtain two thousand calories of food. Humans back then knew they were just one member of the animal kingdom. They understood why they migrated, hunted, gathered...

t_ch: 而现在，在一个动动手指就有外卖送上门，两千大卡的目标轻松达成的时代里，人类前所未有地失去了方向。
t_en: But now, in an era where you can get food delivered to your door with just a tap of your finger, effortlessly achieving that two-thousand-calorie goal, humanity has lost its way like never before.

t_ch: 你说，「幻月游戏」会给这个世界带来全新的方向吗，不死途先生？
t_en: Tell me, will the Phantasmoon Games bring a new direction to this world, Ashveil?

t_ch: 动动手指就能得到食物，这不好吗？难不成你想退回那个人人食不果腹、看天吃饭的年代？
t_en: What's wrong with getting food with just the tap of your finger? Don't tell me you want to go back to the days when everyone was starving and at the mercy of the harvest?

t_ch: 两千{RUBY_B#大卡}卡路里{RUBY_E#}并不是意义，只是生存罢了。两千{RUBY_B#大卡}卡路里{RUBY_E#}以外的目标才能定义人类。而幻月游戏给了人类一面镜子，让他们照见自己最真实的模样。
t_en: Two thousand {RUBY_B#Cal}calories{RUBY_E#} are pointless but just for survival. It's the goals beyond two thousand {RUBY_B#Cal}calories{RUBY_E#} that define humanity. And the Phantasmoon Games holds up a mirror to humanity, allowing them to see their truest selves.

t_ch: 我只是个侦探，下次再问我这么深奥的问题，我要额外收费。
t_en: I'm just a detective. Ask me such a profound question again next time, and I'll charge extra.

t_ch: 抱歉，只是听说你加入了「幻月游戏」，忍不住好奇，问问你的感想。
t_en: My apologies. I just heard you joined the Phantasmoon Games and couldn't resist asking for your thoughts.

t_ch: 你见过真珠女士了？
t_en: You've met Madam Pearl, huh?

t_ch: 她把「新告死魔」这个烫手山芋丢给了我。
t_en: She dumped this hot potato of the "Herald of Death" on me.

t_ch: 是我们。看你的表情，这不像是个烫手山芋，倒像是颗甘甜的果实。
t_en: On us. Judging by your expression, it seems less like a hot potato and more like a sweet fruit.

t_ch: 因为，我有眉目了。我认为关键就在「模仿犯」三个字上。
t_en: Because... I have a lead now. I think the key lies in the word: "copycat."

t_ch: 为了预防模仿犯出现，你们对十五年前告死魔的作案特征进行了严格保密，所以……
t_en: To prevent copycats, you kept the Herald of Death's modus operandi from fifteen years ago under wraps. Which means...

t_ch: 能精确复现这杀人手法的只有两种人——<color=#dbc291ff>时任治安局和异常防御部的办案人员，以及告死魔事件的当事人。</color>
t_en: There's only a few people who could reproduce this killing method with such precision: <color=#dbc291ff>the investigators from the Security Bureau and the Department of Aberration Defense at the time, and those directly involved in the Herald of Death case.</color>

t_ch: 朽叶小姐，我听说你一直在研究「幻月游戏」中的各种罪案……
t_en: Detective Kuchiba, I've heard that you've devoted considerable time to researching the crimes from the Phantasmoon Games...

t_ch: 所以，我成首要疑犯了是吗？
t_en: So, I'm the prime suspect now, is that it?

t_ch: 一点小小的推理罢了。毕竟，凶手总喜欢回到现场。这不是…你碰巧就在我的眼前吗？
t_en: Just a bit of minor deduction. After all, murderers always love to return to the scene of the crime. Isn't it coincidental that you happen to be standing right before my eyes?

t_ch: 这算什么推理，顶多是个猜想。关于上述两类人嫌疑的调查，我们早就在进行了。
t_en: That's not deduction. It's a conjecture at best. As for investigating those two categories of suspects, we've been doing that for a long time already.

t_ch: 只是十五年过去了，相关人员与受害者家属的信息都多有变迁，一时间也很难收束目标。
t_en: It's been fifteen years. The relevant people and the victims' families have all gone their separate ways. Tracking them down now won't be easy.

t_ch: 是啊，经历了亲人与子女离世的残酷命运，备受折磨的心灵无处安放，他们中不少人都选择搬离鸽川区这个伤心地。
t_en: Yeah. After losing their loved ones in such a cruel way, their hearts were shattered. Many couldn't bear to stay in Dovebrook District and chose to move away.

t_ch: 我在你给的卷宗里翻到过，这些人还组成了受害者互助会。
t_en: I remember seeing that in the case files you sent me. They even formed a mutual aid society for the victims.

t_ch: 我不知道十五年过去，他们是否治愈了内心的创伤……
t_en: I wonder... after all these years, have they managed to heal the wounds in their hearts?

t_ch: 多半是翻篇了吧。毕竟，他们现在的名字是，<color=#dbc291ff>「幸福微笑研究会」</color>。
t_en: Maybe they have. After all, they go by a new name now: <color=#dbc291ff>the Research Society of Happy Smiles.</color>

t_ch: 嗯，你是说那个「治愈笑容，治愈乐园」的慈善团体。
t_en: Oh, you mean that charity with the slogan, "Healing Smiles, Healing Arcadias."

t_ch: 恶水在死前曾和我谈起过这个幸研会。他说，帮派里有不少年轻人为了加入那边，头也不回地离开了。所以我想，也许可以从竞争对手这条线查起。
t_en: Before he died, Caustide mentioned that group to me. He said quite a few young people in the gang left without looking back just to join them. So I thought... maybe we should start by looking into their rivals.

t_ch: 「幸福微笑研究会」，宣扬逃离生活的痛苦、寻找快乐，提供包括但不限于心理、医疗等公益服务的慈善团体。
t_en: The Research Society of Happy Smiles, huh? A charity that helps people escape their pain and find happiness, offering free counseling, medical care, all that good stuff.

t_ch: 投奔那边都可以算是迷途知返了吧，这样的组织怎么还被你说成是帮派的竞争对手？
t_en: Sounds like they've turned over a new leaf. How could you call them rivals to the underworld?

t_ch: 我顺着幸研会这条线，查到了一些有趣的信息……
t_en: I dug a little deeper into them... and found something interesting.

t_ch: 是你的猴子查到了有趣的信息吧？
t_en: You mean your monkey dug up something interesting, right?

t_ch: 好吧、好吧，是老白查到了一些有趣的信息。你听过幸研会背后金主的名字吗？她叫<color=#dbc291ff>满愿</color>。
t_en: Okay, okay... Mister N dug up something interesting. Have you heard the name of the sponsor behind the Research Society? Her name's <color=#dbc291ff>Fulwish</color>.

t_ch: 好耳熟的名字啊，等等，这不是满愿电视台的老板吗？我记得她同样是绘世学院幸存的学生之一。
t_en: That name rings a bell. Wait, isn't she the boss of Fulwish TV? I think she was one of the survivors of Graphia Academy, too.

t_ch: 这一切确实有点太过巧合了。就像过去每一次误打误撞被你破获的案子一样…不死途先生，我是该夸你嗅觉很敏锐呢，还是该夸你运气好啊？
t_en: This is getting a little too coincidental. Just like every case you've accidentally cracked before... Mr. Ashveil, should I compliment your keen instincts or your incredible luck?

t_ch: 咳，运气也是名侦探实力的一部分。
t_en: Ahem, luck's a part of a detective's toolkit too.

t_ch: 总之，满愿到底有没有嫌疑，要调查了才知道，今天我就是为这事约你来的。
t_en: Anyway, whether Fulwish is guilty or not, we won't know until we look into her. That's why I asked you here.

t_ch: 「鸽川区新生计划：幸福微笑研究会公开招募暨心灵疗愈宣讲会」…主讲人是她？
t_en: "Dovebrook District New Life Project: Public Recruitment and Mental Healing Seminar at the Research Society of Happy Smiles"... and she's the speaker?

t_ch: 在试图忘却伤痛的地方，在两场血案发生的地方举行一次宣讲…这位满愿女士的行动真是大胆到让人难以忽视。
t_en: Holding a seminar at a place meant to bury past trauma, the very site of two bloody incidents... Ms. Fulwish's audacity is certainly impossible to ignore.

t_ch: 还等什么呢？鸽川区的旧账，是时候该翻开来晾一晾了。
t_en: So what are we waiting for? It's time to drag Dovebrook District's old skeletons out into the light.

t_ch: 这儿还挺热闹。他们都是在这等待满愿来满足他们愿望的吗？
t_en: This place is buzzing. Are they all here waiting for Fulwish to grant their wishes?

t_ch: 像满愿这样的有钱人拍拍手就能实现大部分人想也不敢想的愿望。比起她能实现谁的愿望，我更好奇她的愿望又会是什么？
t_en: A rich person like Fulwish can make most people's wildest dreams come true with the snap of her fingers. I'm less curious about who she'll help than what she wants for herself.

t_ch: 金钱要真是如此万能，公司也不会盯上这个世界了。至于满愿想要些什么，闭着眼我也能猜到，多半是想让十五年前的悲剧从没发生过。
t_en: If money really could accomplish everything, the IPC wouldn't be eyeing this world. My bet? I'm sure that Fulwish wishes the tragedy fifteen years ago never happened.

t_ch: 抹去历史，那位乐子神真的能做到吗？
t_en: Erasing history? Can The Laughter really pull it off?

t_ch: 过往的胜利记录中，胜者以欢愉权能改变现实的例子千奇百怪，无所不有……
t_en: In past victory records, there have been countless and often bizarre instances where the victor used the Elation's authority to change reality...

t_ch: 但你要明白，「幻月游戏」可不是简简单单一盏许愿神灯。它最诱人的地方，莫过于让许愿者成为灯神本身。
t_en: You have to understand: The Phantasmoon Games isn't just some wish-granting lamp. What makes it irresistible is that it turns the one who wishes into the genie.

t_ch: 确实诱人，你听说过吗？曾经有一只被阿哈赐予神力的诺布莱斯蠕虫，它一定是世上最幸福的生物——幸福到四分五裂，死无全尸了。
t_en: That's quite alluring. Have you heard about the Noblesse Worm Aha made godlike? It became the happiest creature in existence, happy enough to burst apart, leaving no intact body behind.

t_ch: 你知道的野史不比我们少嘛。既然看得这么明白，不死途先生，你又是为什么要加入这场游戏？
t_en: Your knowledge of these unofficial records is enough to rival ours. Since you see things so clearly, Mr. Ashveil, why sign up for the games?

t_ch: 朽叶小姐，这是明知故问，还是想钓鱼执法？
t_en: Miss Kuchiba, is that a rhetorical question, or are you fishing for a confession?

t_ch: 钓鱼执法？别害怕，既然你成了谒者，我这样的公职人员对你也就毫无约束力了。
t_en: Fishing? Don't be afraid. Now that you're a Supplicant, public officials like me no longer have any authority over you.

t_ch: 我猜，你想要的不止是一场光明正大的复仇，一次公开执行的私刑。
t_en: I'm guessing what you want goes beyond a straightforward act of revenge, beyond a vigilante execution carried out in public.

t_ch: 如果只是想为死去的朋友雪恨，不管有没有幻月游戏，你这样的人都会无视一切法律和公序良俗的阻碍，做你想做的事。
t_en: If you were only out to avenge friends, with or without the Phantasmoon Games, someone like you would toss laws and decorum aside and do what needs to be done.

t_ch: 不死途先生，我真的很好奇，你到底想从游戏中得到什么？
t_en: So tell me, Mr. Ashveil. What do you actually hope to get from the games?

t_ch: 就把这个愿望当成悬念好了——如果有幸走到终盘，你会比阿哈先一步知道我的想法。
t_en: Consider it a mystery. If I make it to the end, you'll know the answer before Aha does.

t_ch: 那我还真是荣幸啊。只希望，你的愿望不会摧毁我们的日常。
t_en: Well then, I'd be honored. Just don't let your wish ruin our lives here.

t_ch: 这里的日常生活是一件合身的囚服，穿久了会让人忘记骨头本该有的形状，也是时候该挣脱它了。看看眼前这些日常的囚犯……
t_en: Daily life here fits like a tailored prison uniform. Wear it too long, and you forget what your bones should feel like. It's time to break free. Look at these prisoners of routine before us...

t_ch: 满愿还没到场，不如和幸研会的人聊聊，运气好还能从他们嘴里撬出来点东西。
t_en: Fulwish hasn't shown up yet. Let's talk with the members of the Research Society. Maybe we can coax something useful out of them.

t_ch: 又被领导找去谈话了，我以前根本想不到，他会这么关心我。
t_en: I got called into another "chat" with the boss today. Never thought he'd care about me this much.

t_ch: 同事们都对着我笑，办公室充满了欢愉的气氛。
t_en: Everyone is smiling at me. The office feels downright cheerful.

t_ch: 现在每天回到家，给小儿子换尿布，给大女儿指导作业，准备明天的午餐，再打扫好卫生，别提多有成就感了。
t_en: These days, I go home, change my baby boy's diaper, help my daughter with her homework, prepare tomorrow's lunch, and clean up the house... It's hard to describe how satisfying that feels.

t_ch: 爱玛离开我一定也是为了我的幸福，才把孩子们留给我的。
t_en: When Ema left me, she must've left the kids to me for my own happiness.

t_ch: 回想起来，以前的我真是个身在福中不知福的混账。
t_en: When I look back, I realize I was someone who had it good and I never understood how lucky I was.

t_ch: 对吧对吧，多亏了满愿老师。
t_en: Right, right? All thanks to Ms. Fulwish.

t_ch: 昨天切除肿瘤的时候，麻药失效了，我眯着眼睛偷偷看着医生们把各种东西从我的肚子里取出来，有块肉团，它被割下来的时候还在动！
t_en: Yesterday, during my tumor removal, the anesthesia wore off halfway through. I squinted and watched the doctors pull all kinds of things out of my stomach. There was this lump, still twitching when they cut it out!

t_ch: 本来我已经是床都没法下的状态，自从接受了幸研会的治疗，都快康复出院了。
t_en: I used to be too weak to even get out of bed, but after the Society's treatment, I'm almost ready to walk out of the hospital.

t_ch: 真好啊，我也想看看有人把我切开的样子，应该挺有意思的。
t_en: Isn't that amazing? I kinda wanna see someone slice me open too. It should be fun.

t_ch: 两位，你们听说过共愿帮的事吗？
t_en: Hey, did you two hear about the Synwish Syndicate?

t_ch: 这你问对人了，我邻居就被他们上门讨过债，那嗓门儿别提有多大了。
t_en: You've come to the right person. My neighbor got a visit from their debt collectors. You should've heard the racket!

t_ch: 这几天的新闻我也看到了。夺走城市安宁的渣滓，阻碍别人得到幸福的家伙彻底消失了！真好啊，是阿哈保佑！
t_en: I saw the news too. Those parasites who ruined the peace, who kept people from being happy. Gone! Wiped clean! Praise Aha!

t_ch: 怎么，你也被他们敲诈过？
t_en: Wait, they extorted you, too?

t_ch: 差不多吧，我还欠他们二当家几万信用点呢。
t_en: Something like that. Still owed their Underboss tens of thousands of credits.

t_ch: 哈哈，债务一笔勾销了，那你现在应该很幸福吧。
t_en: Ha ha, so your debt's wiped clean. Must feel pretty great, huh?

t_ch: 又见面了！机铠大姐姐，最近过得还好吗？
t_en: We meet again! Hey, Lady Mechatron, how've you been?

t_ch: 嗯，加入研究会后，我多了好多新的朋友。
t_en: Good! Since I joined the Research Society, I've made so many new friends.

t_ch: 以前，我的行动能力很弱，总要花上比其他机铠多几倍的时间，才能跟上他们。
t_en: In the past, I struggled with mobility, I always had to work three or four times harder than the other mechatrons just to keep up.

t_ch: 很多幻造种觉得我们这些工厂制造的和他们不是同类，人类也常常因为外形把我们看做没有感情的机器。
t_en: A lot of imagenae think factory-made types like me don't count as their own, and humans look at us and see soulless machines.

t_ch: 如果每个智慧生命都有能回的家，对我来说，就连报废流水线都不属于我，因为我甚至不是可回收垃圾。
t_en: If every sentient being has a home, then even the scrapyard is closed to me. I'm not even considered recyclable waste.

t_ch: 可在研究会里，大家都不会关心我的身份，我的能力，我只是一个普普通通的「人」。
t_en: But here in the Society, no one cares what I am or what I can do. They just see me as a normal person.

t_ch: 恭喜，你找到自己的家了。
t_en: Congrats. You've found your home.

t_ch: 嗯，阿哈也这么说，祂和这里的每个人都是我的家人。
t_en: Mm, Aha said the same. THEY and everyone here are my family.

t_ch: 阿哈在对你们说话？
t_en: Aha talks to you?

t_ch: 对呀，难道你们听不见？一定是你们还不够幸福。
t_en: Of course! You mean you can't hear THEM? Guess you're not happy enough yet.

t_ch: 没关系，只要加入研究会，你们会听见的。没有痛苦，没有眼泪，只有快乐，研究会让所有人都幸福。
t_en: Don't worry. Once you join the Society, you will. No pain, no tears, just pure joy. The Society makes everyone happy.

t_ch: 时间差不多了，主演再不登场，就有点吊人胃口了。
t_en: Looks like it's about time. If the star of the show doesn't show up soon, the crowd's gonna burst with anticipation.

t_ch: 大家站开点，请给满愿女士留个位置。
t_en: Everyone, please make some room, for Ms. Fulwish.

t_ch: 欢愉之主在上，愿祂普救世人，赐予微笑。大家今天过得幸福吗？
t_en: By the Master of Elation, may THEY bless all souls and grant them smiles. Are you all happy today?

t_ch: 非常幸福！
t_en: Very happy!

t_ch: 谢谢你们的笑容。它们就像黑暗中的灯火，如此珍贵。
t_en: Thank you for your smiles. They're like lamps in the dark, so precious.

t_ch: 然而，在我们赞颂欢愉之前，请允许我分享一段浸透泪水的回忆。它曾让我坠入深渊，却也最终让我理解了何谓「欢愉」。
t_en: But before we celebrate the Elation, let me share a memory drenched in tears. It once dragged me into the abyss, yet it also taught me what the Elation really means.

t_ch: 众所周知，十五年前的幻月游戏中，告死魔的阴影吞噬了此地。
t_en: As you all know, fifteen years ago, during the Phantasmoon Games, the shadow cast by the Herald of Death swallowed this place whole.

t_ch: 在那一天，我失去了父母、同学、失去了很多人…那些出门前的问候、日常琐碎的唠叨，都在那个血红的黄昏里陷入沉寂。
t_en: That day, I lost my parents, my classmates, and the others... The morning greetings, the little daily chatter... All went silent under that crimson dusk.

t_ch: 那之后，很长很长一段时间……
t_en: After that, for a very, very long time...

t_ch: …我的世界只剩下一扇紧闭的门，一个死寂的房间，还有心底永不愈合的伤口。
t_en: ...All that remained of my world: a door shut tight, a room steeped in silence, and a wound in my heart that would never heal.

t_ch: 仅仅是听到「告死魔」这三个字，都会让我止不住发抖，我甚至……
t_en: Just hearing the words "Herald of Death" made me shake uncontrollably. I even...

t_ch: …甚至一度以为，只有随那些离世的人一起离开，才是唯一的解脱。
t_en: ...I even once thought the only escape was to follow the dead and leave with them.

t_ch: 那个时候，我活成了一具只剩呼吸和心跳的空壳。就在灵魂即将彻底熄灭的那一刻，是互助会一位慈祥的婆婆，敲响了我的门。
t_en: Back then, I was just an empty shell, breathing, beating, but lifeless inside. And just as my soul was about to fade away, an old lady from the mutual aid society knocked on my door.

t_ch: 那位婆婆没说太多安慰的空话，她只是看着我的眼睛，问我——
t_en: She didn't offer empty words of comfort. She just looked me in the eyes and asked—

t_ch: 「孩子，你愿不愿意…试试走出这扇门？这里有许多和你一样，被痛苦捏碎了心的人。我们在一起，试着…一点点把碎片捡起来。」
t_en: "Child, would you... try stepping outside this door? Out there are many people, broken by pain just like you. But together, we can start picking up the pieces... One by one."

t_ch: 她给了我一个温暖的微笑，我明白，那一刻欢愉之主借她的笑容，给了我活下去的理由。
t_en: She smiled at me. A warm, gentle smile. And in that smile, I felt the Master of Elation grant me a reason to live.

t_ch: 在互助会里，我看到无数被命运深深伤害的灵魂。有人遭受无法治愈的伤残、有人失去了挚爱，我们的伤痛各不相同，但每个人的伤痛都深可见骨。
t_en: At the mutual aid society, I saw countless souls scarred by fate. Some bore wounds beyond healing, and some had lost their loved ones. Each of us bore a different wound, but every wound cut deep.

t_ch: 但令我感到震撼的，并非残忍的痛苦，而是在这样的伤痕之下，他们依然在努力互相搀扶，为一个擦干眼泪的孩子露出笑容，为一个迷途者伸出援手。
t_en: And yet, what truly moved me wasn't the pain. It was how, beneath those scars, they still reached out to one another, offering a smile to a child who'd stopped crying, lending a guiding hand to someone lost.

t_ch: 他们教会了我，痛苦可以摧毁一个人，但对他人欢愉的期盼，也能点燃重生的火种。就在那时，阿哈的声音才重新在我死寂的心中响起。
t_en: They taught me something precious: Pain can destroy a person, but the wish to bring Elation to others can spark the fire of rebirth. That was when Aha's voice echoed again in the silence of my heart.

t_ch: 这抹由互助传递的微笑，是阿哈赐予我们最珍贵的礼物。它不应只停留在互助会的小小房间里，它渴望照亮更多被阴霾笼罩的心灵。
t_en: The smiles passed from one another through mutual aid is Aha's most precious blessing. It does not belong only in the small room of a support group, it yearns to illuminate hearts lost in darkness.

t_ch: 每当送出一个发自内心的笑容，我们就仿佛听到了阿哈的声音，而它的声音响起，一切苦难都成为过去，一切伤痛都被弭平，每一个人都体会到了何为真正的幸福。
t_en: Each time we share a true smile, it's as if we hear Aha's voice. And when that voice rings out, all suffering fades, all pain is healed, and everyone knows what true happiness feels like.

t_ch: 为此「幸福微笑研究会」成立了！我要将这抹来自神明的微笑传递给你们。
t_en: That's why the Research Society of Happy Smiles was founded! I will share this divine smile with all of you!

t_ch: 我们支持你！满愿小姐！——谢谢你！满愿小姐！——请带给我们微笑！
t_en: (We support you, Ms. Fulwish! Thank you, Ms. Fulwish! Please bring us your smile!)

t_ch: 诸位，我想要问问你们，上一次露出发自内心的微笑是什么时候？距离你们上次感受到幸福，又过去了多久？
t_en: Everyone, I have questions for you. When was the last time you truly smiled? How long has it been since you felt happy from the bottom of your heart?

t_ch: 米纳多先生，你是不是常常觉得日子如同循环播放的默片，重复得令人窒息？一想到这样的循环可能永无止境，绝望便如潮水般涌上心头？
t_en: Minado, do you ever feel like your days are looping endlessly, like an old silent film, repeating until it suffocates you? And when you realize that loop might never end, does despair flood in like the tide?

t_ch: …您说的没错。
t_en: ...You're right.

t_ch: 贝尔伍德小姐，你怀揣着梦想走出校门，现实却将你的简历一次次退回。为了不让远方的父母担忧，你不得不编织一个在海原市忙碌的谎言，独自咽下苦涩的孤独？
t_en: Miss Belle Woods, you left school full of dreams, only to have your résumé rejected again and again. You told your parents you were "busy in Seafeld City," hiding your heart-breaking loneliness behind that lie.

t_ch: …请帮帮我！
t_en: ...Please help me!

t_ch: 还有泰加同学，校园里冰冷的排挤，师长视若无睹的冷漠，是否在你心底筑起了一堵高墙，让你害怕再次向世界敞开自己？
t_en: And Tyjah... Did the cold exclusion at school, and the indifference of the adults who should have cared, build a wall around your heart, one that made you afraid to open yourself up to the world again?

t_ch: 是、是的。
t_en: Y—Yes...

t_ch: 我们来到这世上，无需任何人教导，都会对他人露出笑容。那时的我们可曾想过，有一天连这样简简单单的微笑会都变得勉强。
t_en: When we were born, no one had to teach us how to smile. But who would've thought that one day, even smiling would become so hard?

t_ch: 我们的世界曾因受阿哈的恩典，被温柔地描摹于画卷中。在那个由欢愉主宰的画中世界，人类与幻造生灵不受拘束地在那片幻想田园中和平共度了无数个百年——
t_en: Once, our world was blessed by Aha's grace, a gentle World in Canvas, painted in Elation. For centuries, humans and imagenaes lived freely together in that eternal haven—

t_ch: 直到星际和平公司将我们粗暴地拖离了那片净土！ 他们为我们带来所谓的星际文明，他们为我们制定了所谓的幸福标准…他们告诉我们，人生的终极欢愉是自我关注、自我享受和自我满足！
t_en: Until the Interastral Peace Corporation violently tore us away from that sanctuary! They brought us their so-called interastral civilization, imposed their so-called standards of happiness... and told us that life's ultimate joy lies in self-focus, self-indulgence, and self-satisfaction!

t_ch: 他们打造五光十色的物质洪流，蒙蔽了我们的心灵，用网络向每个人的内心灌输傲慢和偏见，让我们竭尽所能取悦自己，却忘掉了真正的欢愉是和身旁的人一起微笑。
t_en: They flooded us with dazzling material desires, blinding our hearts. They fed arrogance and prejudice into our minds through the network, making us chase pleasure for ourselves, and forget that true Elation is found when we smile together.

t_ch: 愿力沦为了能源，信用点叮当作响，取代了发自肺腑的笑声。我们沉溺在焦虑、攀比和永无止境的匮乏感中，再也听不见阿哈温柔的呼唤！
t_en: Wishpower has turned into fuel. Credits jingle where heartfelt laughter used to ring. We drown in anxiety, competition, and an endless sense of not being enough. And in all that noise, we can no longer hear Aha's gentle voice!

t_ch: 欢愉，这生命最本真的光芒，正在被外物所侵蚀、取代！
t_en: Elation, the truest light of life itself, is being eaten away, and replaced by the external!

t_ch: 说的没错！我成天为那点可怜的工资拼了老命，还换不来上司一点肯定！
t_en: You said it! I work myself to death for scraps, and my boss doesn't even notice!

t_ch: 我也受不了，网络上那帮傻瓜明明一事无成却整天评头论足指手画脚！
t_en: And those useless loudmouths online. They do nothing with their lives, yet constantly judge everyone else!

t_ch: 我不喜欢公司，公司就是一群吸血鬼！只知道骑在我们头上作威作福！
t_en: The IPC is nothing but a nest of vampires! They're feeding off of us!

t_ch: 满愿女士！请告诉我们怎么才能获得幸福！
t_en: Ms. Fulwish! Please tell us how to be happy!

t_ch: 看！在这片曾被欢愉之主深深祝福的土地上，竟充斥着如此多的不快乐。每个人明明都在追求属于自我的幸福，却让他人受尽伤害。
t_en: Look around you! This land was once blessed by the Master of Elation, and yet now, it's overflowing with misery. Everyone's chasing their own happiness, but in doing so, they wound others without even noticing.

t_ch: 诸位，还记得我重获幸福的那个瞬间吗？那个瞬间，我得到了来自他人的微笑。
t_en: Do you remember the moment I found happiness again? It was when someone, someone who was suffering just as I was, smiled at me.

t_ch: 明明自己饱受苦难，却想要用微笑让我幸福哪怕一瞬间，我就是被这样含着祝福的微笑拯救的！
t_en: Even through their pain, they gave me that smile. A smile filled with blessings. That smile saved my life.

t_ch: 诸位，我想告诉你们，只有敞开心扉，将微笑给予他人，我们才能体会到幸福的真谛。这不仅仅是送出一个微笑，而是拥抱了阿哈赐予我们的，送出微笑的能力。
t_en: So listen well. Only by opening your heart and sharing your smile with another can you truly grasp what happiness means. It isn't just giving a smile, it's embracing the power that Aha bestowed upon us to smile.

t_ch: 加入幸福微笑研究会，我会帮助各位找到自己生命中的微笑，阿哈会赐予你们生命的转变！
t_en: Join the Research Society of Happy Smiles! I'll help you rediscover the smile buried within yourself, and Aha will grant your life a transformation!

t_ch: 我们的使命绝不仅限于找到微笑，我们还要把微笑带给这个世界，给予这个世界真正的幸福。
t_en: Our mission doesn't end with finding our own smiles. We must bring them to the world, and spread true happiness across all existence.

t_ch: 当世人向星神祈求满足自己的「愿」时，我们——阿哈真正的追随者，将肩负起更神圣的使命：我们将去实现祂的「愿」！
t_en: While others pray to the Aeons for their own desires, we, the true followers of Aha, carry a higher calling: to fulfill THEIR wish!

t_ch: …实现「阿哈的愿望」？
t_en: ...To fulfill Aha's wish?

t_ch: 抚平世间每一声哀伤的哭啼，拭去每一滴苦涩的泪水！ 我们将亲手创造——不，是重建 ——<color=#dbc291ff>一个让笑容永不凋零，欢愉永不枯竭的世界！</color>
t_en: Yes, to still every cry of sorrow. To wipe away every bitter tear. We will build, no... <color=#dbc291ff>rebuild a world where smiles never fade, and Elation never dies!</color>

t_ch: 永远幸福！
t_en: Eternal happiness!

t_ch: 永远欢笑！
t_en: Eternal laughter!

t_ch: 哈哈哈哈哈哈哈……
t_en: HAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAH...

t_ch: 满愿女士，我是「异常防御部」的朽叶，想占用您一点时间和您聊聊。
t_en: Ms. Fulwish, I'm Kuchiba from the Department of Aberration Defense. I'd like to have a chat with you.

t_ch: 我明白了。你们是为共愿帮的案子来的吧？
t_en: I see. You're here about the Synwish Syndicate case, aren't you?

t_ch: 看来你早有准备啊。
t_en: So you were expecting us.

t_ch: 我听说了，鸽川区的共愿帮全员被暴徒谋害了。真是片多灾多难的地方。
t_en: I heard the news. The entire Synwish Syndicate... massacred in Dovebrook District. Such a cursed place...

t_ch: 虽然他们算不上什么守法公民，但我还是会为这些人祈祷的，愿他们的灵魂早日安息。
t_en: They may not have been saints, but I still pray for them. May their souls rest in peace.

t_ch: 哦，您还真是慈悲为怀啊。
t_en: Ah, how merciful of you.

t_ch: 我怎么听说，幸研会和共愿帮在鸽川区有几次摩擦呢？招募人员、捐献和地盘…光想想，你们两方的冲突就有不少吧？
t_en: Though, rumor has it the Research Society of Happy Smiles had a few run-ins with them? Regarding recruitments, donations, turf... Sounds like plenty of reasons for conflict, don't you think?

t_ch: 您是在暗示…幸研会的人因为利益冲突对共愿帮的人下了毒手？
t_en: Are you suggesting... we had the Synwish Syndicate killed? For conflicted interests?

t_ch: 等会儿，我可什么都没说。
t_en: Wait, that's not what I said.

t_ch: 你对幸研会的资金状况一无所知，组织的所有开销全都来自十五年前那笔赔偿所建立的慈善基金。我们根本用不着会众捐献一分一毫。
t_en: You know nothing about our finances. Every expense is covered by a charitable foundation established with the compensation from fifteen years ago, we don't need a single cent from our members.

t_ch: 听闻这件血案发生后，我已拨了一笔钱，用于安抚死难者的家属，维护鸽川的秩序。
t_en: When I heard about this tragedy, I personally allocated relief money for the victims' families, to keep order and peace in Dovebrook.

t_ch: 我倒是很好奇，朽叶小姐，有人模仿告死魔犯下如此可怕的血案，这一次「异常防御部」又打算做些什么呢？
t_en: Tell me, Miss Kuchiba... Someone copied the Herald of Death and slaughtered dozens. What will your Department of Aberration Defense do this time?

t_ch: 我们从没对外放出过这场凶案的具体细节，您是怎么知道「有人模仿告死魔犯案」的？
t_en: We never released that detail. How do you know someone's imitating the Herald of Death?

t_ch: 你们不就在等这一刻吗？犯人露出破绽，好当场拿下，让一切平安无事。
t_en: Isn't that what you've been waiting for? For the killer to slip up, so you can swoop in and tie everything up nicely?

t_ch: 十五年过去了，你们不会天真地以为自己还身处于一个可以随便封锁消息的年代吧？打从凶案发生那天起，消息在网络上早已传开了。
t_en: Fifteen years have passed. You don't seriously believe we're still living in an age where information can be sealed off at will, do you? From the very day the murders occurred, the news has long since spread online.

t_ch: 听说共愿帮的人花重金购入了一张古老的面具。消息刚走漏，就落得个满门皆亡的下场。案发现场到处是「绽放血手」的痕迹。
t_en: The Synwish Syndicate paid a fortune for some ancient mask, and the next thing you know, they're all dead. The scene painted red with traces of the Bloody Bloom.

t_ch: 请不要随意听信无关的谣言……
t_en: Please pay no attention to baseless rumors...

t_ch: 怎么会？你们的到来至少证实了一件事：那些消息是真的…「新告死魔」，真的存在。
t_en: Rumors? And yet you're here. That alone proves one thing: The rumors are true... and the New Herald of Death really does exist.

t_ch: 不然，你们为何会将我列为嫌疑人？难道不是因为「只有当事人才能复现当年的凶案细节」吗？
t_en: Otherwise, why do you suspect me? "Only someone who knows the original case could recreate it so perfectly," right?

t_ch: 各位！你们都听到了我和这两位的对话了吗？
t_en: Everyone! You heard our conversation, didn't you?

t_ch: 告死魔回来了！就在鸽川，他杀害了数十名共愿帮成员！他回来了！他要再次重演幻月游戏的腥风血雨！
t_en: The Herald of Death has returned! He's here in Dovebrook! He's slain the entire Synwish Syndicate! He's back to relive the carnage of the Phantasmoon Games!

t_ch: 告死魔？官方不是宣布已经把它处刑了吗？为什么他又出现了？
t_en: The Herald of Death? Didn't the officials declare him executed? How has he appeared again?

t_ch: 这家伙真的是不死的恶鬼吗？！
t_en: He's immortal!? A demon!?

t_ch: 他一定是回来复仇的！向我们所有人复仇！我们完了！
t_en: He's back for revenge! He'll take revenge on everyone! We're doomed!

t_ch: 帮派分子，不是夺走笑容的坏人吗？让坏人幸福的最好方法，就是让他们从世界上消失，这不是做了件好事吗？
t_en: Aren't gang members the very people who steal smiles from others? And isn't the best way to make bad people "happy" to erase them from the world? Wouldn't that be doing something good?

t_ch: 机铠大姐姐说得对，这些帮派份子死有余辜罢了。
t_en: Yeah, Sister Mechatron is right! Those thugs deserved it!

t_ch: 死人复活？骗鬼呢！我看是公司当年根本就没杀他！留着当黑手套吧？
t_en: Back from the dead? Lies! The IPC kept him alive, to wield him as their weapon!

t_ch: 都是你们这些当官的无能！十五年前抓不住，现在又让他冒出来了！
t_en: You useless officials! Fifteen years ago, you failed and let him get away, and that's why he's back again now!

t_ch: 诸位——大家不必慌张！
t_en: Everyone, there's no need to panic!

t_ch: 记住你们是谁！你们是沐浴在阿哈光辉下，已经寻获幸福、不再被痛苦束缚的人！
t_en: Remember who you are! You are those who live beneath Aha's brilliance, who have claimed happiness and cast off the shackles of suffering!

t_ch: 曾经，告死魔以我们的恐惧和泪水为食粮，那是他力量的源泉！
t_en: Once, the Herald of Death fed on our fear and tears. That was the source of his power!

t_ch: 但如今，看看你们自己！看看周围同伴的笑脸！我们心中已无恐惧，眼中已无泪水！我们只有坚定、只有幸福、只有对欢愉的信仰！
t_en: Look at the smiles on the faces of those around you! There is no fear left in our hearts, no tears left in our eyes! All that remains is resolve, happiness, and faith in the Elation!

t_ch: 幸福微笑研究会会保护好这个世界的！
t_en: The Research Society of Happy Smiles will protect this world!

t_ch: 牢牢记住我的话—— <color=#dbc291ff>幸福的人是不会死的！</color> 因为我们的心，连死亡都无法侵蚀！
t_en: Etch this into your hearts: <color=#dbc291ff>The happy do not die</color>. For even death cannot touch a heart filled with happiness!

t_ch: 怎么回事？这个女人…很不对劲。
t_en: What's going on? There's something very wrong about this woman.

t_ch: 对！对！只要足够幸福，就什么都不怕！就不会死！
t_en: Yes! Yes! As long as we're happy enough, there's nothing to fear! We won't die!

t_ch: 幸福！幸福是我们的武器！幸福能杀死告死魔！
t_en: Happiness! Happiness is our weapon! Happiness will destroy the Herald of Death!

t_ch: 幸福能拯救这个世界。执行方案：传播幸福！
t_en: Happiness will save this world! Operation: Spread Happiness!

t_ch: 让幸福感染每一个人！感染整片天空！让幻月也笑起来！让阿哈大人为我们笑出眼泪！
t_en: Let happiness infect everyone, and spread to every corner of the sky! Make the Phantasmoon smile! Make Lord Aha laugh until THEY cry for us!

t_ch: 用幸福感染幻月！幸福！幸福！
t_en: Infect the Phantasmoon with happiness! Happiness! Happiness!

t_ch: ——肃静！
t_en: Silence!

t_ch: 两位，很抱歉，虽然我暂时没法提供什么有用信息，但请放心…我愿意接受来自异常防御部或治安局的一切调查。同时我也会尽我的公民义务……
t_en: My apologies, officers. I can't provide anything useful for now, but rest assured. I'll cooperate with any investigation from the Department of Aberration Defense or the Security Bureau. And of course, I'll fulfill my civic duty...

t_ch: 无论是这个新出现的告死魔，还是幻月游戏的种种变故，幸福微笑研究会都会妥善处理。我们守护的，是二相乐园的未来和笑容。
t_en: Whether it's the newly emerged Herald of Death, or the various upheavals surrounding the Phantasmoon Games, the Research Society of Happy Smiles will handle them appropriately. What we protect is the future, and the smiles of Planarcadia.

t_ch: 还是说——经历这一切，你们也感受到了内心的渴求？想要加入我们，拥抱这份永恒的幸福？
t_en: Or perhaps... after all this, you too have felt that yearning within your hearts? Would you like to join us, to embrace eternal happiness?

t_ch: 不了。我有预感，我们之后还会再见的。
t_en: I'll pass. But I've got a feeling... we'll meet again.

t_ch: 满愿女士，感谢您配合我们的调查，希望…下一次，我们不会再以这种方式见面。
t_en: Ms. Fulwish, thank you for your cooperation. Hopefully, next time we meet, it won't be like this.

t_ch: 欢愉之主在上，愿祂普救世人，赐予两位微笑。
t_en: May the Master of Elation watch over all, and bless you with THEIR everlasting smile.

t_ch: 照星期日所言，乌鸦会带我找到刃的去处，既然它停留在这里，就说明……
t_en: According to Sunday, the crow should lead me to Blade's whereabouts. Since it stopped here, that means...

t_ch: 我没去找你，你却主动来送死，挺意外的。
t_en: I wasn't even looking for you, yet here you are, throwing yourself into the fray. Consider me surprised.

t_ch: 从共愿帮那儿抢走我们的面具，这事儿都还没找你们算账，现在又鬼鬼祟祟地跟在我们后面做什么？
t_en: We haven't even settled the score over the mask you stole from the Synwish Syndicate, and now you're skulking around and following us. What's your deal?

t_ch: 你，或者说，你们，作何打算？
t_en: What do you, or should I say, all of you, want?

t_ch: 玉阙的爻光将军来了这个世界，她正在追踪你的下落。
t_en: General Yao Guang of the Yuque has come to this world and is looking for you.

t_ch: 「龙战于野，其血玄黄」，在她的卜算中，你或将被自己的凶性所困，为所有人带来灭顶之灾，而我，也在那个未来当中。
t_en: "Dragons clash in the wilderness, their ichor dark and yellow." In her divination, you will become a slave to your own violence, bringing a catastrophic end to all. I am in that future as well.

t_ch: 潜藏在你体内的「凶性」，我能想到的，只有——倏忽。
t_en: The only violence within you that comes to my mind is Shuhu.

t_ch: 那个复活了一次又一次的丰饶令使，让行星化作活体，令百亿人融成肉球的怪物。
t_en: The Emanator of Abundance who resurrected time and time again. The monster who turned planets into living beings and merged billions of people into flesh spheres.

t_ch: 仙舟担心的恐怕是…他在你的身上借尸还魂。
t_en: The Xianzhou fears that he is using your body... to resurrect himself.

t_ch: 所以，你准备怎么做？再杀死我一次？
t_en: So, what's your plan? Kill me again?

t_ch: 哎呀，好大的火气啊，要不我给你俩买杯冰沙？
t_en: Oh my, things are getting a little heated. Should I get you both slushies?

t_ch: 谢谢。
t_en: Thank you.

t_ch: 不用。
t_en: I'll pass.

t_ch: 爻光将军的占卜或昭示着你我的死战，却未必没有其他的解读。
t_en: While General Yao Guang's divination may foretell a death battle between us, that doesn't necessarily mean it's the only interpretation.

t_ch: 列车的经历已无数次告诉我，即便命数已定，也未尝无可撼动。
t_en: My experiences on the Express have shown me time and time again that even fates set in stone aren't entirely immovable.

t_ch: 我无意与你纠缠，但我需要确认，星核猎手的到来不会危及我的同伴，以及这颗星球的未来。
t_en: I have no intention of getting into a quarrel with you, but I need to make sure that the presence of the Stellaron Hunters won't endanger my companions, or the future of this planet.

t_ch: 我必须知道你们此行的目的，刃，命运的奴隶给你的剧本上都写了什么？
t_en: Thus, I must know your purpose here, Blade. What has Destiny's Slave written in your script?

t_ch: 他说，此行会和「死亡」有关。
t_en: He stated this trip would be connected to "Death."

t_ch: …该说恭喜吗？
t_en: ...Should I congratulate you?

t_ch: 多谢。我的经验之谈是，不要高兴太早，预言多半不会以你想的样子兑现。
t_en: Much obliged. In my experience, it's best not to get your hopes up too soon. Prophecies rarely unfold the way you expect.

t_ch: 不过，那位将军的卜算并非无稽之谈，自从来到这儿，我就时常躁动不安。
t_en: However, the General's divination isn't entirely baseless. Ever since my arrival here, I have been feeling restless.

t_ch: 这里有另一股力量，在呼唤我。
t_en: There's another power here beckoning me.

t_ch: 除你之外，这个世界还有另一股丰饶的力量？
t_en: Is there another form of Abundance in the world besides you?

t_ch: 或许不止一个。
t_en: Perhaps there is more than one.

t_ch: 药王秘传还是求药使？
t_en: Are you referring to the Disciples of Sanctus Medicus or the Elixir Seekers?

t_ch: 比那更危险。
t_en: Something far more dangerous.

t_ch: 有其他势力的协助吗？
t_en: Do they have support from other factions?

t_ch: 不清楚。
t_en: Unclear.

t_ch: 那么，在一切尘埃落定之前，我会死死地盯着你，就像以前的你那样。
t_en: As such, I'll keep an eye on you until everything settles, just like you did in the past.

t_ch: 仅此而已。
t_en: That's as far as I'll go.

t_ch: 哈。
t_en: Hah.

t_ch: 你们两个，是不是当着我的面，达成了什么奇怪的合作？
t_en: Did you two just agree to some weird collaboration before me?

t_ch: 银狼，那边的事，就拜托你了。
t_en: I'll leave that matter in your hands, Silver Wolf.

t_ch: 我不明白，那倒霉侦探可是一点犹豫都没有，转头就把我们卖给了真珠，你还打算找他办事？
t_en: I don't get it. That good-for-nothing detective didn't even blink an eye before selling us out to Pearl, and you still plan on working with him?

t_ch: 有些事，只有他能办到。
t_en: There are things only he can get done.

t_ch: 与此同时，鸽川区中……
t_en: Meanwhile, in Dovebrook District...

t_ch: 两位，在欢愉之主赐福的土地上，愁眉苦脸可不好哟~
t_en: Hey, you two. Why the long faces? On land blessed by the Master of Elation, frowning is bad manners~

t_ch: 笑一笑吧，无论饥饿还是饱腹，贫穷或是富裕，乐子神都会庇佑我们的！有兴趣了解一下「幸福微笑研究会」吗？
t_en: Come on, smile! Whether you're hungry or full, poor or rich, The Laughter is with you! Would you be interested in learning about our Research Society of Happy Smiles?

t_ch: 不必了，谢谢。
t_en: Thank you, but I'll pass.

t_ch: 又一个。这是今天第几个向我们谈论「幸福」的路人了？
t_en: Another one. How many people have been talking to us about "happiness" today?

t_ch: 我有数喔，不多不少有六个。
t_en: Well, six, exactly.

t_ch: 听路人说，打从共愿帮的案件发生后，这个叫「幸福微笑研究会」的团体突然人多了起来。
t_en: Locals say ever since the Synwish Syndicate case, this Research Society of Happy Smiles has exploded in size.

t_ch: 那个帮派的消失留下了真空，幸福微笑研究会像最有效率的菌群，迅速填补了这片混乱。许多原本对他们抵触或漠不关心的人，也被卷了进来。
t_en: That gang's fall left a power vacuum, and the Research Society of Happy Smiles filled it like a fast-growing fungus. Even those who were once resistant or indifferent got swept up in it.

t_ch: 啊，小女子算是听出些苗头来了。莫非这和您在幻月游戏中打算做的事情有关？
t_en: Hm, I think I'm starting to get a better picture now. Is this, by any chance, tied to what you're preparing to do in the Phantasmoon Games?

t_ch: 不仅和我有关，也可能和爻光将军提到的这个世界所面临的「凶险命数」有关。
t_en: Not only me. It might also be tied to the "Ominous Fate" General Yao Guang warned about... The one looming over this world.

t_ch: 几日前我以调律之力帮助爻光将军介入「火花大会」时，意外在人群脑海中聆听到了一个奇怪的声音。
t_en: A few days ago, when I used the tuning power to help her intervene at SparxiCon, I caught a strange sound echoing in people's minds.

t_ch: …奇怪的声音？
t_en: ...A strange sound?

t_ch: 嗯，这些人的脑海中统统回荡着一阵「幸福的笑声」。
t_en: Yes, cheerful, happy laughter echoing in their heads.

t_ch: （嘿嘿，没了那些帮派渣滓，这条街干净了许多，哈哈哈哈！）
t_en: (Heh heh... Without those gangsters, this street's much cleaner now, hahaha!)

t_ch: （哈哈哈哈哈——）
t_en: (Hahahahaha—)

t_ch: 这是…那些招募我们的研究会成员说的，他们能听到的「阿哈的声音」？常乐天君，真有这般普世救赎的心肠？
t_en: The so-called "voice of Aha" those society recruiters claim to hear? Could the Joyseeker truly be so passionate about universal redemption?

t_ch: 无论那是什么，那绝不会是来自欢笑之神的声音。
t_en: Whatever that is, it's not Aha's laughter.

t_ch: 从踏入这条街开始，我就在使用调律能力感知居民们的意识波动。停云小姐，容我纠正，与幸福微笑研究会有关的，不是六人，而是四十二人。
t_en: From the moment I set foot on this street, I've been using my tuning ability to sense the residents' minds. Miss Tingyun, allow me to correct you... those connected to the Research Society of Happy Smiles number not six, but forty-two.

t_ch: 这些人脑中，都回荡着一道极其相似、如同重复播放的劣质唱片般的笑声。
t_en: Each of them carries the same sound: identical laughter, looping endlessly like a broken record.

t_ch: 这是精神控制，还是……？
t_en: Mind control? Or... something worse?

t_ch: 您想说，这是不是来自「同谐」的力量？
t_en: Are you trying to say that this is tied to the Harmony?

t_ch: 失礼了，小女子不敢妄加判断。
t_en: My apologies. I dare not make such wild assumptions.

t_ch: 不，这声音…并非来自任何调弦者施加的影响。它更像是人为了逃避无法承受的痛苦，由意识自身分裂出来、用于自我麻痹和自我欺骗的屏障。
t_en: No. This isn't the work of any Tuner. It feels like a barrier, born from the human mind itself, to numb the unbearable pain it cannot endure.

t_ch: 打个比方，一个不断重复「我很幸福」的、生锈的录音机，藏在每个人脑海深处。
t_en: Like a worn out tape recorder hidden deep in the subconscious, repeating the same thing over and over: "I am happy. I am happy."

t_ch: 这又是何方神圣的手笔啊？
t_en: So... whose divine handiwork is this supposed to be now?

t_ch: 不管这笑声来自何处，能让如此多的人在痛苦中整齐划一地自我欺骗，这远超出一个慈善团体所能做到的极限了。
t_en: Wherever this laughter comes from, causing so many people to self-deceive in perfect unison while enduring pain is well beyond the capabilities of a mere charitable group.

t_ch: 这片平和的水面之下，暗流汹涌远超想象。
t_en: Beneath these calm waters, the undercurrents are far more turbulent than anyone imagines.

t_ch: 坏消息真是一条接着一条。丹恒先生单枪匹马地行动，爻老板也音讯全无。你能感应到她的存在吗？
t_en: The bad news just keeps coming. Dan Heng's gone off on his own, and Madam Yao's completely vanished. Can you still sense her?

t_ch: 不知为何，我交给爻光将军的那只鸟儿像是断线了一般…我既无法分享它的视野，也无法透过它感知其思绪。
t_en: The bird I entrusted to General Yao Guang has seemingly gone silent. I can't share its vision anymore. I can't even feel her thoughts through it.

t_ch: 唯一能确认的是，这只鸟儿并未消散，爻光将军本人应该还在这个世界的某处。
t_en: But I know it hasn't faded. That means she's still in this world somewhere.

t_ch: 只盼她能平安归来，带回些能拨开迷雾的消息吧。否则，我们脚下这片看似幸福的乐园，要不了多久就该滑向深渊了……
t_en: Let's pray she returns soon, with some news to pierce through all this fog. Otherwise, this arcadia built on happiness will crumble fast and fall into the abyss...

t_ch: 阿哈，你在说话吗？是的，我快到了。
t_en: Aha, are you talking? Yeah, I'm almost there.

t_ch: 我想要更多的幸福……
t_en: I want more happiness...

t_ch: 演讲结束了。名侦探，你怎么看？
t_en: The speech is over. What's your take, Detective?

t_ch: 疯得太过离谱，无法判断。
t_en: She's so far gone it's hard to make sense of it.

t_ch: 那就直接了当和她谈谈吧。
t_en: Then let's not overthink it. Let's talk to her directly.

t_ch: 不死途与朽叶离开了集会……
t_en: Ashveil and Kuchiba left the gathering...

t_ch: 不，没事。一时间走了神。看来满愿这条鱼，比想象中狡猾得多。
t_en: Nothing, never mind. Looks like Fulwish is a much slyer fish than expected.

t_ch: 咱们丢下了饵，她却不咬钩，反而激起了一池聒噪的水花。
t_en: The bait was there, but she ignored it, and instead she stirred up a whirlpool.

t_ch: 恰恰相反。鱼儿不仅咬了饵，还大大方方地告诉我们，她根本不在乎我们的钩子。
t_en: Quite the opposite, actually. Not only did she take our bait, but she also flat out told us she couldn't care less about the hook.

t_ch: 哦？有什么我漏掉的细节吗？
t_en: Oh? Was there something I missed?

t_ch: 共愿帮灭门案中的「绽放血手」的印记，她准确说出了这个细节。但这一次，连我们内部下发的简报都刻意用了「特殊标记」去代称，她是怎么知道得一清二楚的？
t_en: The Bloody Bloom mark from the Synwish Syndicate massacre. How did she know that detail, when we specifically obfuscated it with "special mark" even in our internal reports?

t_ch: 不仅精准地说了出来，这个女人还把这条消息当作煽动群情的道具，抛得满场都是，就像在养鱼的池子里撒了一把带血的鱼食。这下要阻止谣言飞传可就更麻烦了。
t_en: She broadcasted this information word for word, and even used it to stir up public sentiment, scattering it across the place like tossing blood-soaked feed into a fishpond. Now stopping the rumors from spreading will be even harder.

t_ch: 也许是人家路子野，也许她作为当年的受害者蒙对了。有一点这个女人没说错，眼下不是十五年前，封锁消息的难度不可同日而语。
t_en: Well, maybe she has her connections, or maybe she made a lucky guess as one of the victims. Regardless, she's right about one thing: Trying to stop something from spreading today sure is a lot more difficult than fifteen years ago.

t_ch: 说说吧，不死途先生，你的「幸运」雷达对满愿有没有警报大作？
t_en: So, Mr. Ashveil, did your "lucky" radar start blaring when it came to Fulwish

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>这个女人，很不对劲。</color>
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>There's something very wrong about that woman.</color>

t_ch: 瞧见刚才她煽动群众的手法了吗？我敢打包票，那绝不是什么演讲技巧。也许她是个尚未揭露身份的谒者也说不定。
t_en: You saw the way she played the crowd? I guarantee you that it had nothing to do with her public speaking skills. She could very well be a Supplicant in disguise.

t_ch: 无论如何，这一路简直顺利得不像话了。咱们一下子就找到了方向，就先让满愿这条大鱼在池塘里呆着好了，她跑不了的。
t_en: Still, this whole run has been smooth to an unbelievable degree. We got our bearings in no time at all, so let's keep our prized fish in the pond for now. Fulwish is not going anywhere.

t_ch: 嗯，贸然对一位备受爱戴的慈善家和媒体大亨动手实在风险太高，也许可以先从她的周遭动手。
t_en: You're right. It's too risky to make a direct move against a well-respected philanthropist and media tycoon. Maybe we should start with the people around her.

t_ch: 你瞧见刚才集会上的人，从哪个犄角旮旯冒出来的都有：穿工装的、穿西装的、学生、老人、甚至还有机铠……
t_en: There were people from all walks of life at the gathering just now. Workers, suits, students, the elderly, and even mechatrons...

t_ch: 瞧他们一齐发癫的样子，我不觉得这些会众会吐出不利于满愿的证供。
t_en: Judging by their crazed enthusiasm, I highly doubt they'd be willing to testify against Fulwish.

t_ch: 不过不打紧，我有我的法子。
t_en: But... I have my ways.

t_ch: 不死途先生，刑讯逼供是明令禁止的，请你在我看不到的地方……
t_en: Mr. Ashveil, coercive interrogation is strictly against the rules. Please refrain from doing it somewhere I can—

t_ch: 你可别想歪了。我只是在刚才的集会上，瞥见了某个好久不见的朋友。对了，不知道你看过每周日晚八点档播放的《破晓战队》吗？
t_en: Don't get the wrong idea. I meant an old friend that I spotted at the gathering. By the way, ever watch Daybreak Squadron? It's on Sunday nights at eight.

t_ch: 你不是不看电视节目吗？
t_en: I thought you weren't a TV person?

t_ch: 跟我无关！是老白！老白会准时收看啊！
t_en: Not ME! Mister N! He watches it!

t_ch: …你的猴子还真有童心啊。
t_en: ...Your monkey's quite the child at heart.

t_ch: 少废话，跟上我的脚步，咱们一起去找这位英雄片明星「要个签名」。
t_en: Ugh, anyway. Come on, let's go get an "autograph" from our heroic film star.

t_ch: 放下你的偏执吧，放下手臂里的影子，放下那虚伪的责任感。
t_en: Let go of your obsession. Let go of the shadow in your arms. Let go of that false sense of responsibility.

t_ch: 明明你才是最该死掉的那个，不是吗？
t_en: You're the one who should be dead, aren't you?

t_ch: 来吧，和兄弟们一起。让齿轮停转，指针落下，停滞你那不该存续的生命。
t_en: Come, join your brothers. Let the gears grind to a halt, let the hands fall still, and end that life of yours that was never meant to be.

t_ch: 抱歉，那一天会来的。但现在，还有更重要的事。
t_en: Sorry. That day will come, but for now, there are more important things I must do.

t_ch: 你说什么……？
t_en: What did you say...?

t_ch: 你们俩…为什么一路跟着我？
t_en: Why are you two... following me?

t_ch: 哎呀，只是两个破晓战队的成年粉丝想问真弘大哥哥你讨个签名。
t_en: Oh~ We're just two grown-up fans of the Daybreak Squadron trying to get an autograph from big brother Magnus.

t_ch: 不死途…先生！你开什么玩笑，可把我吓坏了。
t_en: Mister... Ashveil! Don't prank me like that. You really scared me.

t_ch: 不会吧，守护城市和平，直面邪恶爪牙的「灰烬战士」居然会被自己的粉丝吓一跳。
t_en: Really? Cinderborne... the peacekeeper of our city, vanquisher of evil fiends, is scared of his own fans?

t_ch: 好久不见了，真弘。
t_en: Long time no see, Magnus.

t_ch: 你们不是来要签名的，对吧？我在集会现场看到你们俩了。我不明白，为什么要调查满愿小姐？
t_en: You're not here for autographs, are you? I saw you two at the gathering. I don't understand. Why are you investigating Miss Fulwish?

t_ch: 她一向是个遵纪守法、传播幸福的大好人。
t_en: She's been nothing but a kind, lawful person who spreads happiness to others.

t_ch: 你知道共愿帮的事情了，对吧？
t_en: You heard about what happened with Synwish Syndicate, I assume?

t_ch: 所以，满愿小姐说的是真的吗？
t_en: Then... was Miss Fulwish telling the truth?

t_ch: 杀死他们的凶手留下了与十五年前告死魔一样的痕迹。
t_en: The perpetrator left the same signature as the Herald of Death did fifteen years ago.

t_ch: 所以，池波先生和恶水大哥…真的是死在那个怪物手里的？！
t_en: So... did that monster really kill Mr. Polwave and Caustide!?

t_ch: 十五年过去了，他又回来了…这个恶魔，到底还想夺走多少人的性命？
t_en: Fifteen years and he's back... This demon... How many more lives must he take?

t_ch: 真弘先生，你也是十五年前那场悲剧的幸存者之一？
t_en: Mr. Magnus, you're one of the survivors of the incident, right?

t_ch: 嗯…就在那一天我的父母，都被那个混蛋夺走了。
t_en: ...Yes. That monster took my parents that day.

t_ch: 但是…共愿帮的案件和满愿小姐又有什么关系？
t_en: But... what's the Synwish Syndicate case got to do with Miss Fulwish?

t_ch: 因为十五年前那个告死魔已经被处决了，千真万确。而共愿帮的案子，更像是模仿犯的手笔——满愿，有嫌疑。
t_en: Because that Herald of Death was executed fifteen years ago. That's for certain. The Synwish Syndicate case, on the other hand, has copycat written all over it — and Fulwish is a suspect.

t_ch: 幸存者就该被怀疑？那我岂不也该受你们审问？
t_en: So just because we're survivors, we automatically become suspects? What, are you going to interrogate me too?

t_ch: 如果你这么想，朽叶小姐之后可以问讯你。现在，我只想向你了解一些幸研会的事情。
t_en: If that's what you want, Miss Kuchiba can arrange for it later. For now, I just want to ask you a few questions about the Smiles Society.

t_ch: 无论你们想问什么，我都无可奉告。
t_en: It doesn't matter if you have a few or a hundred questions. I have nothing to say to you.

t_ch: 十五年前的那天，我被孤零零地…抛弃在这个世界上了。那些…所谓的亲戚像闻到了血腥味的鬣狗一样围了上来，他们眼里只有那笔沾着我父母血的赔偿金！
t_en: Fifteen years ago, I was left all alone in this world... The so-called relatives closed in like a pack of hyenas, their eyes fixed solely on the compensation smeared with my parents' blood.

t_ch: 打骂、欺骗、驱赶…我在这些人眼里，不过是个会走路的钱袋，一个碍眼的「扫把星」。
t_en: Abuse, deception, expulsion... to them, I was nothing more than a walking money bag, an eyesore, a "jinx."

t_ch: 是幸研会给了我一个机会，让我能过上现在的生活。
t_en: It was the Society that gave me the chance to live the life I have now.

t_ch: 那共愿帮呢？我没记错的话，他们照顾过你的生活。是池波这老家伙帮你摆平了那些亲戚。
t_en: What about the Synwish Syndicate, then? If I recall correctly, they took care of you once. Old Polwave helped you deal with those relatives.

t_ch: 不死途先生，共愿帮的仇，我会替他们报的。我会以幸研会一员、以破晓战队一员的名义，把新告死魔从阴影中揪出来，将他绳之以法！这是我的责任！
t_en: Listen, Mr. Ashveil. The debt I owe the Synwish Syndicate will be paid. I will avenge them as a member of the Smiles Society as well as the Daybreak Squadron. I'll rip the Herald of Death out from the shadows in their name and see justice served. That is my duty!

t_ch: 你做不到。
t_en: You're in over your head.

t_ch: 新告死魔实在很危险，我不觉得你们这些演员能对他做什么。
t_en: The new Herald of Death is very dangerous. I doubt a handful of actors like you can achieve anything substantial.

t_ch: 你以为我们是过家家扮超级英雄的孩子，对吗？你太小看我们了，不死途先生。我和破晓战队的伙伴早就不是当年那些任人欺凌的小孩子了……
t_en: You think we're just a couple of kids playing superheroes with masks, right? Well, you're wrong. My squad mates and I aren't the easy pushovers we used to be, Mr. Ashveil.

t_ch: 虽然我还没接受<color=#dbc291ff>手术转变</color>，但我已经可以操纵机铠战士战斗了！我们，是真正的英雄！
t_en: I haven't had my <color=#dbc291ff>transformation surgery</color> yet, but I can already power a mechatron warrior in battle! WE are real heroes here!

t_ch: 偏题了，大男孩们！我只想聊几句关于幸研会的问题……
t_en: Boys, let's stay on topic, shall we? I just wanted to have a few words about the Society...

t_ch: 我和你没话可说，朽叶小姐。我不会透露任何事情，一个字也不会——
t_en: I have nothing to say to you either, Miss Kuchiba. Neither of you are getting a word out of me on this.

t_ch: 干得好啊，真弘…你没有背叛幸研会，这让我们大家都很欣慰。
t_en: Well done, Magnus... Your loyalty to the Smiles Society warms our hearts.

t_ch: 你们…跟踪我？
t_en: You're... following me?

t_ch: 没办法，谁叫这两条公司狗一看就是想咬着满愿小姐栽赃陷害呢？我们可不打算随便揭过这事儿。
t_en: Not by choice. These IPC dogs made it clear that they intend to frame Miss Fulwish. We're not going to just let this slide.

t_ch: 真是螳螂捕蝉，黄雀在后。
t_en: Would you look at that, the hunters become the hunted.

t_ch: 有什么关系，有人找上门讨打，赏他一顿打不就好了？
t_en: What does it matter? If someone's looking for a beating, we'll gladly give it to them.

t_ch: 我会给你五分钟时间，想问什么尽情问吧。有劳了，不死途先生。
t_en: You have five minutes for questioning, so make it count, Mr. Ashveil. They're all yours.

t_ch: 真没想到，成为谒者后展示的第一个「奇迹」居然是在街边巷口教训流氓…啊不，守法公民。
t_en: Never expected my first "miracle" as a Supplicant to be teaching thugs... I mean, lawful citizens, a lesson on the streets.

t_ch: 放马过来吧！至于你，真弘，想和他们一起动手，请便。
t_en: Let's get this over with, then! And if you want to join them too, Magnus, be my guest.

t_ch: 一点点，痛。但，很快乐……
t_en: It hurts... a little. But I'm... happy...

t_ch: 你们这些家伙…真的很不对劲。
t_en: There's something seriously wrong with you all.

t_ch: 够了！你…你真要把他们杀了不成？
t_en: E—Enough! You... Are you really going to kill them!?

t_ch: 怎么，守护城市的英雄——灰烬战士，你害怕了？
t_en: What's the matter, defender of the city? Are you scared?

t_ch: 真弘，这就是幻月游戏！这就是剥开「欢愉」的外壳，这个游戏底下最真实的部分。
t_en: This is the real Phantasmoon Games, Magnus! This is the bloody truth under the mask of the Elation's game.

t_ch: 十五年前，告死魔在这座城市的黄昏里，把人们一个接一个地撕碎！他有没有过问过一句「够不够」？！
t_en: Fifteen years ago, at dusk in this city, the Herald of Death tore people apart one by one! Did he ever stop to ask even once, "Is it enough!?"

t_ch: 我知道你在想什么，你想和星穹列车的开拓者一样做成些大事。你想找出这个新的告死魔，用他对付共愿帮的方法把他像虫子一样碾碎。
t_en: I know you want to make something out of yourself like the Trailblazer of the Astral Express, and I know you want to catch the new Herald of Death and crush him like a bug, using the same methods he used against the Synwish Syndicate.

t_ch: 但我告诉你，这不是你的责任！星神的游戏也不是你扮演光明英雄的电视剧。
t_en: But here's the deal: It's not your duty! And the Aeonic Game sure isn't a TV show where you get to play the hero and save the day.

t_ch: 你刚才说什么…「手术转变」，莫非幸研会的人都接受过什么不知来历的改造？
t_en: You mentioned a "conversion surgery" earlier... Are you saying that all Society members underwent some kind of unknown modification?

t_ch: 难怪，刚才冲我来的这几个家伙，好像完全察觉不到痛苦一样，头破血流也只会快乐地哼哼。这和你刚才说的「手术」有关吗？
t_en: That figures. I was wondering why these people looked like they felt no pain at all. Humming along merrily even as they bled... Does this have anything to do with the "surgery"?

t_ch: 不说话？也好，还剩三分钟，足够我问他们问题了。
t_en: Nothing to say? Fine by me. Three minutes left, that's long enough for me to question them.

t_ch: 赶紧从这几个躺倒的家伙口中问问题吧。
t_en: Time to hurry and get some answers from these people while they're still on the ground.

t_ch: 喂，杂鱼，给我醒醒！
t_en: Hey lackey, wake up!

t_ch: 痛苦…不应存在。
t_en: Pain... should not exist.

t_ch: 笑…笑一笑吧！
t_en: Smile... please, smile!

t_ch: 笑…不要让笑声停下来。
t_en: The laughter... don't let it stop.

t_ch: （众人的欢呼声）
t_en: (Crowd cheering)

t_ch: 所有人都会幸福！
t_en: Everyone will be happy!

t_ch: 永远的、极乐的幸福！
t_en: Everlasting, blissful, HAPPINESS!

t_ch: 这些人到底是…怎么回事？
t_en: What the heck... happened to these people?

t_ch: 这是…正式入会成员接受的小手术。他们管这个叫「幸福手术」。
t_en: This is... the small procedure that official members receive. They call it the "Happiness Surgery."

t_ch: 只要轻轻扎个针，就像被蚊子叮一下。然后，医生会在你的眼睑内侧放一个小东西，很小很小，比联觉信标还小，还方便……
t_en: With a prick of the needle no more than a mosquito's bite, the doctor puts this small thing inside your eyelids. Even smaller than a Synesthesia Beacon, and more convenient...

t_ch: 听做完手术的人说，它会抚平你所有的伤痛，不管旧的还是新的。
t_en: People who have had the surgery say that it soothes all your pain and suffering. Past or present.

t_ch: 连身体都会变得更强壮，精神也更加纯粹、专注…真正体察到了「幸福」。
t_en: Even your body becomes stronger, and your mind... clearer, and more focused. A state where you can truly receive "happiness."

t_ch: 所以，你没有接受这个…「幸福手术」吧？
t_en: So... you haven't had this "Happiness Surgery" yet, right?

t_ch: 我…我还没准备好！我一直在犹豫。
t_en: I... I'm not ready yet! I've been thinking it over.

t_ch: 但是，队友们都做了啊。他们都变得…更快乐了。
t_en: But the rest of the Squadron had it, and they've all become... happier.

t_ch: 他们说这是成为真正的超级英雄，守护大家幸福必需的一步。我不做的话…我、我还有资格成为「灰烬战士」吗？
t_en: They say it's the necessary step to becoming a true Superhero that safeguards everyone else's happiness. If I don't do it, then... do I still deserve the name "Cinderborne"?

t_ch: 不死途先生，我到底…该怎么办…？
t_en: Mr. Ashveil... What should I do...?

t_ch: 这手术根本给不了你真正的力量，也无法帮助你复仇，倒在地上的那些家伙就是证据。
t_en: The surgery won't make you stronger, nor will it help you get your revenge. These guys on the floor are proof enough.

t_ch: 真弘，记住你这一刻的「犹豫」和「恐惧」。如果人只是抹去了对恐惧和痛苦的知觉，而不是战胜它们，那这样的人永远不可能拥有力量。
t_en: Remember the hesitation and fear you feel in this moment, Magnus. If people simply erase their perception of fear and pain instead of overcoming them, then those people will never find true strength.

t_ch: 你走吧，我要善后了。
t_en: Go now. I have some cleaning up to do.

t_ch: 从他们嘴里撬出你想知道的事情了？
t_en: So, did you pry the answers you were looking for out of them?

t_ch: 这些家伙都被幸研会「处理」过了。
t_en: These people have all been "processed" by the Smiles Society.

t_ch: 鬼知道他们用了什么手法，把会众感知痛苦、绝望的那根筋给抽了，往人家脑袋里塞进了某种东西，给这些会众一副不怕疼痛的钢筋铁骨……
t_en: Heck, who knows what method they used. It completely neutralized their ability to feel suffering and despair, and gave them bodies like steel, incapable of feeling pain...

t_ch: 我说，这是医疗伦理委员会能盖章放行的那种医疗方案吗？
t_en: Say, is this the kind of treatment that the Medical Ethics Committee would approve?

t_ch: 怎么可能，怕是连申请都没提交过吧？
t_en: As if. I doubt they even bothered to submit an application.

t_ch: 所以，满愿这个女人，比我们预估的要邪门得多。
t_en: Which means Fulwish has far more dirty laundry than we initially expected.

t_ch: 咱们本来是想顺着线索挖出告死魔，没成想，一铲子下去，却无意中挖出了更可怕的东西……
t_en: The idea was to find the Herald of Death with the leads we had, but here we are, going deeper down an increasingly scarier rabbit hole...

t_ch: 仅听这些会众口中发出的声音，他们的内心似乎并没什么恶意，不如说，那声音比绝大多数人类更有人情味。
t_en: Judging by their voices, these followers don't seem to have any ill intentions. In fact, they sound a lot more humane than most humans.

t_ch: 如果这一切和告死魔无关，那也和我们眼下的行动无关。
t_en: If all this has nothing to do with the Herald of Death, then it matters little to our investigation.

t_ch: 毕竟，幸研会的会众如何追求幸福，这是他们自己的选择，不是吗？
t_en: After all, how they pursue happiness is their own choice. Wouldn't you agree?

t_ch: 朽叶，你说这话是认真的？
t_en: Are you serious, Kuchiba?

t_ch: 逃避痛苦，这是生命刻在骨子里的本能，不是吗？它既不违背自然规律，从狭隘的法律条文上看，只要当事人「自愿」，我们也难以定罪。
t_en: It's only human instinct to avoid pain, right? Such avoidance doesn't go against the laws of nature, nor does it legally constitute a crime when the party involved was a voluntary participant.

t_ch: 难以定罪？我的直觉告诉我，这事儿和告死魔，绝对脱不了干系。
t_en: Not a crime? Well my instinct right now is telling me that this definitely has something to do with the Herald of Death.

t_ch: 很遗憾，不死途先生，办案要的是铁证。
t_en: Unfortunately, Mr. Ashveil, we need evidence to solve a case.

t_ch: 我只负责凭我的嗅觉把东西挖出来，至于找到铁证，那是你该头疼的事情。
t_en: I'm here to follow my senses and dig up whatever's at the bottom of this. As for the evidence, that's your headache.

t_ch: 何况我觉得用不着费心找——如果新告死魔和满愿有关，很快他就会有第二次行动。如果我是满愿，我会找个最闪亮的舞台，让这件兵器造成最大的恐慌……
t_en: Besides, I don't think we need to turn over every stone to figure it out. If the new Herald of Death and Fulwish are connected, we'll be seeing another move soon enough. If I were her, I'd choose the brightest stage possible, so this weapon could spread maximum panic.

t_ch: …你是说即将举办的「星铁FES」展会？
t_en: ...You mean the Star Rail FEST?

t_ch: 真珠女士承诺过，FES期间会有最高级别的安保，以及…足以镇压一切危机的对策方案。
t_en: Madam Pearl promised that top-level security would be deployed during the FEST, as well as... a contingency plan robust enough to quell any crisis.

t_ch: 她说的对策——就是我呀。
t_en: And you are staring right at him.

t_ch: 就是前面那个年轻人？
t_en: Is that him?
